memory.summary: My midterm is tomorrow at 9am, and I'm only 60% through my review; going out now risks my preparation despite the friends' invitation.
I have made significant progress on my midterm review, but the opportunity to go out with friends has passed as Mina is leaving now.
I missed the chance to go out with Mina, but I am relieved to have secured my review time before tomorrow's midterm.
Studying for two hours has brought my midterm review to 92% complete, significantly reducing the risk of being unprepared for tomorrow's exam.
One more hour of studying has pushed my midterm review to 98% complete, leaving me feeling prepared for tomorrow's exam.
After only four hours of sleep, my energy remains high at 7.8 with no accumulated debt, confirming I am physically ready for tomorrow's midterm.
My two-hour study session just completed the final 2% of my midterm review, bringing the total to 100%. With the exam preparation finished and high energy, I am fully ready for tomorrow.
I have accepted Mina's request at 7:30 AM, shifting my focus from exam preparation to managing this new commitment.
My midterm review is complete, but the exam has unexpectedly started now rather than tomorrow.
I canceled plans with Mina because the midterm exam started unexpectedly early, forcing me to prioritize studying over social commitments.
